summary: Verified fix for /api/images/products/{filename} Content-Type bug. Endpoint now returns correct mime-type based on file extension (image/jpeg for .jpg, image/webp for .webp, image/png for .png). Backend: 4/4 pytest cases passed. Frontend: JPG and WEBP uploaded via admin API rendered successfully in browser (Image.onload fired, naturalWidth>0), confirming the browser now accepts and decodes the served bytes.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/products.py serve_product_image now correctly derives media_type from suffix — good fix. Consider also serving HEAD requests and adding ETag for better cache validation (nice-to-have).
admin_upload_product_image accepts 'image/jpg' which is not a standard MIME type — harmless but noteworthy.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral: synthesized JPEG/PNG/WEBP via PIL, uploaded via /api/admin/products/upload-image, verified serve, then deleted the resulting files from /app/backend/product_images/.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Serve-endpoint mime-type fix is confirmed working end-to-end. All prior upload-flow coverage from iterations 7-8 remains valid.
rca of the issue: Pre-fix, serve_product_image hard-coded media_type='image/png'. When a user uploaded a JPEG or WEBP (allowed by admin_upload_product_image), the file was saved with the correct .jpg/.webp extension but served as 'Content-Type: image/png'. Browsers detected the mime/content mismatch and silently refused to render, showing only the alt text. Fix maps suffix -> correct mime using a small dict. Verified: (1) pytest test_image_mime.py 4/4 pass, (2) live browser Image() load succeeded for JPG & WEBP from the deployed URL.
